characters.Jordan: Young, stubborn, and defiant; believes his clean record makes him untouchable.
characters.Officer Miller: Strict and high-alert; interprets Jordan’s defiance as a potential threat.

script.exposition: Rapping his heavy flashlight against the driver’s side window as Jordan looks on, appearing bored
script.line: Window down all the way. Hands on the wheel. Now.

script.exposition: Sighing loudly and rolling the window down, but leaving one hand in his lap
Maintaining a strict posture and watching Jordan's movements closely
script.line: I said both hands, son. Put that right hand where I can see it.

script.exposition: Speaking defiantly while keeping his hand out of sight
script.line: It’s in my lap, man. I’m just trying to get my phone. I have rights, you know. Why did you even pull me over?

script.exposition: Shining the light directly into Jordan’s eyes, his voice tightening
script.line: You’re going to keep your hands on that wheel and you’re going to stop reaching into your lap. I don't know what you have down there. Is there a weapon in this vehicle?

script.exposition: Laughing nervously and shaking his head in disbelief
script.line: A weapon? Are you serious? I’m an honor student. I don’t have a weapon. I’m just reaching for my—

script.exposition: Barking the command to interrupt Jordan's movement
script.line: DON'T REACH! Every time you move your hand off that wheel, you're making a mistake. Do you understand me?

script.exposition: Getting angry and suddenly reaching toward the passenger seat to grab his wallet from a bag
script.line: Look, I'll just show you my ID so we can be done!

script.exposition: Stepping back quickly, his hand hovering over his holster in a defensive stance
script.line: STAY STILL! DON'T MOVE! Hands on the ceiling! Get your hands up on the ceiling of the car right now!

script.exposition: Freezing in place, his face going pale as the severity of the situation hits him
script.line: I... I was just getting my wallet. My hands are up. Look. My hands are up.

script.exposition: Keeping a firm tone while giving tactical instructions
script.line: Slowly... use your left hand to open the door from the outside handle. Keep the other hand on the ceiling. If you drop that hand, we’re going to have a very different conversation.

script.exposition: His hands shaking violently as he reaches for the door handle
script.line: Okay. Okay. I'm opening it. Don't... please don't do anything. I'm opening the door.
